tasks.title: Define "Session" for Scandal Magnitude Floor
tasks.overview: The Scandals & Follower Loss proposal (Section 3, Magnitude) states: "No scandal should ever remove more than the player earned in their current session." The term "session" is undefined, and the architect cannot spec the magnitude calculation without a clear definition. This is a small but load-bearing design decision — the meaning of "session" determines what data the magnitude formula reads and how the floor is computed.

related_items.purpose: the proposal containing the magnitude floor rule; load Section 3 (Magnitude) to see the rule in context
the accepted core design; load if needed to verify consistency with idle/offline behavior

acceptance_criteria: Define what "session" means in the context of the magnitude floor rule — provide a clear, unambiguous definition (e.g., "time since the app was opened," "time since the last prestige," or another boundary)
Update Open Question #4 in the scandals proposal with the answer as a sub-bullet

tasks.title: Decompose Accepted Proposals into Technical Plan
tasks.overview: Both accepted proposals (Core Game Identity & Loop, Scandals & Follower Loss) need to be decomposed into concrete, assignable engineering tasks with explicit done-whens.
acceptance_criteria: Decompose both accepted proposals into discrete engineering tasks
Each task has explicit acceptance criteria an engineer can pick up and execute

tasks.title: Core Game State & Data Model Architecture
tasks.overview: Define the complete data model for Click Farm: every entity (generators, platforms, currencies, algorithm state), field types, constraints, ownership, mutation rules, and persistence strategy. This is the skeleton that all implementation hangs off. Per the accepted Core Game Identity & Loop proposal, the key structural commitments are: per-platform follower counts (Platform is a full sub-entity), three currencies at three lifecycle speeds, and a multi-input engagement-to-follower conversion formula.

related_items.purpose: source design — defines all entities, currencies, generators, platforms, algorithm
stretch goal — data model should accommodate scandal accumulators without requiring them at launch

acceptance_criteria: Architecture doc in architecture/ defining every game entity with fields, types, and constraints
Persistence strategy defined — what format, where it lives, how it's loaded/saved
Mutation rules for each entity — who can change what and through which interface
Data model accommodates scandal accumulators as an additive extension (not required at launch but must not be blocked by core model)

tasks.title: Game Loop & Systems Architecture
tasks.overview: Define the game loop execution model, component boundaries, and interface contracts for all core systems: tick/update cycle, engagement-to-follower conversion contract (multi-input formula depending on algorithm state, platform affinity, generator type), algorithm shift system (seeded PRNG per architect review), idle/offline calculation (calculate-on-open), and the state machine for the game loop (normal play, and later scandal_active state). Define what each system exposes to other systems and where the boundaries are.

related_items.purpose: source design — core loop, algorithm, idle rules
stretch goal — PR Response introduces a modal game state; architecture should accommodate this

acceptance_criteria: Architecture doc in architecture/ defining the game loop execution model (tick/update cycle)
Interface contract for engagement-to-follower conversion formula with all inputs named
Algorithm shift system spec: seeded PRNG, shift schedule, fuzzy window, how state is exposed
Idle/offline calculation spec: calculate-on-open formula, what state is snapshotted on close
Component boundary diagram or list — one sentence per component responsibility
Coupling analysis: named dependencies between systems, what breaks if a dependency changes

tasks.title: Implement Core Data Model & Game State
tasks.overview: Implement the core data model as defined in the architecture spec. This includes all game entities (GameState, Generator, Platform, Currency balances, AlgorithmState), their types, initialization, serialization/deserialization, and persistence. This is the foundation every other system builds on.

related_items.purpose: design source — entity definitions, currency model, generator roster

acceptance_criteria: All core game entities implemented with fields matching the architecture spec
Game state can be created (new game), serialized, and deserialized without data loss
Unit tests verify initialization defaults and round-trip serialization
Persistence layer can save and load game state

tasks.title: Implement Click-to-Post & Engagement Pipeline
tasks.overview: Implement the basic click-to-post mechanic: player clicks, a post is created, engagement is generated based on base rates. This is the simplest slice of the core loop — one generator (Selfies), one platform, no algorithm modifiers yet. The goal is a working Post → Engage pipeline that later systems plug into.
acceptance_criteria: Player can click to create a post that generates engagement
Engagement is calculated using the generator's base rate
Engagement accumulates correctly in game state
Unit tests verify click → engagement pipeline with expected values

tasks.title: Implement Generator System
tasks.overview: Implement the full generator system: all 7 base content types (Selfies through Viral Stunts) with base engagement rates, follower conversion rates, upgrade tracks (quality, frequency, platform optimization), and trend sensitivity values. Generators auto-produce engagement when purchased. Unlock thresholds gate which generators are available based on progression.
acceptance_criteria: All 7 base generators implemented with base rates, conversion rates, and trend sensitivity
Generators auto-produce engagement per tick when owned
Upgrade tracks modify generator output correctly
Generator unlock thresholds gate availability based on follower count
Unit tests verify generator output calculations and upgrade effects

tasks.title: Implement Algorithm System
tasks.overview: Implement the Algorithm system: a seeded PRNG that determines a deterministic schedule of algorithm states. Each state has a name, favored/penalized content types, and a fuzzy shift window (base interval ± seeded random offset). The algorithm state modifies generator output via trend sensitivity. Expose current state and a coarse instability indicator for UX. Algorithm state advances offline deterministically via the seed.

related_items.purpose: algorithm design — Section 4, architect Q2 answer

acceptance_criteria: Algorithm states defined with content type modifiers (boost/penalize)
Seeded PRNG produces a deterministic shift schedule
Fuzzy shift window prevents clock-watching
Generator output is modified by current algorithm state via trend sensitivity
Algorithm state can be advanced for any elapsed time (supports offline calculation)
Unit tests verify deterministic schedule from seed and correct generator modifiers

tasks.title: Implement Platform System & Cross-Posting
tasks.overview: Implement the platform system: 3 platforms at launch, each with independent follower counts, content affinity (which generators perform best), and unlock thresholds based on total followers. Cross-posting across platforms with the right content mix creates multiplicative returns. Platform affinity interacts with algorithm state to determine effective generator output per platform.

related_items.purpose: platform design — Section 5, architect Q3 answer (3 platforms at launch)

acceptance_criteria: 3 platforms implemented with independent follower counts
Each platform has content affinity modifiers for each generator type
Platform unlock thresholds gate availability based on total followers
Cross-posting calculates output using generator base rate × platform affinity × algorithm modifier
Unit tests verify platform affinity calculations and unlock progression

tasks.title: Implement Engagement-to-Follower Conversion
tasks.overview: Implement the engagement-to-follower conversion pipeline. This is the multi-input formula that converts accumulated engagement into followers on each platform. Inputs: engagement amount, generator type, platform affinity, algorithm state modifier, generator's follower conversion rate. Followers accumulate per-platform. This is the Engage → Grow beat of the core loop.
acceptance_criteria: Engagement converts to followers per-platform based on the multi-input formula
Conversion respects generator conversion rates, platform affinity, and algorithm state
Follower counts update correctly across all active platforms
The full Post → Engage → Grow loop works end-to-end
Unit tests verify conversion formula with known inputs and expected outputs

tasks.title: Implement Idle/Offline Calculation
tasks.overview: Implement the calculate-on-open idle gains system. When the player opens the game, compute elapsed time since last save, advance the algorithm state deterministically via seed, and calculate pure positive accumulation (engagement and follower gains) for the elapsed period. Per the accepted design: no negative events fire while offline. Snapshot relevant state on close/background.

related_items.purpose: idle rules — Section 8, architect Q4 answer (calculate-on-open)

acceptance_criteria: On game open, elapsed time since last save is computed
Algorithm state is advanced deterministically for elapsed time
Engagement and follower gains are calculated for the idle period (positive only)
Game state is snapshotted on close/background for accurate idle calculation
Unit tests verify idle calculation produces correct gains for known elapsed time and state

tasks.title: Implement Prestige System (Rebrand + Clout)
tasks.overview: Implement the prestige mechanic: Rebranding. Player can rebrand to reset followers and engagement to zero, earning Clout based on total followers at time of rebrand. Clout buys permanent meta-upgrades: faster engagement rates, new generator types (prestige-only), algorithm insight, platform head-starts. The prestige tension is: rebrand now for small Clout or push further for more with diminishing returns.

related_items.purpose: prestige design — Section 7, Clout currency in Section 6

acceptance_criteria: Player can trigger a rebrand that resets followers and engagement to zero
Clout earned scales with total followers at rebrand time
Clout persists across rebrands
At least 4 meta-upgrade types implemented (faster rates, new generators, algorithm insight, platform head-starts)
Meta-upgrades correctly modify gameplay in subsequent runs
Unit tests verify Clout calculation, reset behavior, and upgrade effects

tasks.title: Implement Scandal Risk Accumulator System
tasks.overview: Implement the scandal risk accumulator system as defined in the Scandals & Follower Loss proposal. Each scandal type has an accumulator per relevant scope (generator, platform, or global). Accumulators are event-driven (update on relevant actions), freeze offline, and fire when value exceeds effective threshold + fuzz. Empire scaling decreases thresholds as total followers increase. Expose coarse risk_level (none|building|high) for UX signaling. This is a stretch goal — the core loop must work without it.

related_items.purpose: full scandal system spec — Sections 2-3, architect review for accumulator contract

acceptance_criteria: 6 scandal types implemented with per-scope accumulators (generator, platform, or global)
Accumulators update on relevant player actions (event-driven, not tick-driven)
Empire scaling reduces effective thresholds as total followers increase
Accumulators freeze when offline and resume from frozen state on return
Fuzz uses seeded PRNG consistent with algorithm system
risk_level enum (none|building|high) exposed per accumulator for UX
Unit tests verify accumulator progression, empire scaling, freeze/resume, and fire threshold

tasks.title: Implement Scandal Trigger, Magnitude & Floor
tasks.overview: Implement scandal firing and magnitude calculation. When an accumulator fires: determine magnitude (5-15% of affected platform's followers), apply the session floor (no scandal pushes a platform below its snapshot value from when the app was foregrounded), enforce the ceiling (max 15%), and suppress stacking (only the most severe fires when multiple trigger simultaneously). Deduct followers from the affected platform.

related_items.purpose: magnitude rules — Section 3, session definition in OQ #4 answer

acceptance_criteria: Scandal fires when accumulator crosses threshold, removing 5-15% of affected platform followers
Session floor enforced: follower snapshot taken on app foreground, no scandal pushes below it
Ceiling enforced: no single scandal removes more than 15% from one platform
Stacking suppressed: only the most severe scandal fires when multiple trigger simultaneously
Small cross-platform ripple (1-3%) applied for severe scandals
Unit tests verify magnitude calculation, floor, ceiling, and stacking suppression

tasks.title: Implement PR Response Mechanic
tasks.overview: Implement the PR Response counterplay window. When a scandal fires, the game enters a scandal_active state with a 10-15 second response window. The player can spend Engagement to mitigate damage (more Engagement = fewer followers lost, max mitigation reduces loss to 30-40% of original). The window opens only when the player is present (not during idle). After the timer expires or player confirms, the final magnitude is applied and the game returns to normal state.

related_items.purpose: PR Response spec — Section 4, UX review for interaction pattern

acceptance_criteria: Game transitions to scandal_active state when a scandal fires
10-15 second response window with timer
Player can spend Engagement to reduce follower loss (slider or equivalent input)
Maximum mitigation caps at 30-40% of original magnitude (cannot fully negate)
Spending nothing results in full magnitude hit
Game returns to normal state after timer expires or player confirms
Unit tests verify mitigation scaling, timer behavior, and state transitions
